headline: 3 core contracts identified: Stacks sBTC token (SIP-010), Bitcoin peg UTXO (multisig taproot), and Emily relay API
short_headline: 3 core contracts across Bitcoin and Stacks

findings.text: No block-explorer name-tag search performed: sBTC operates on Bitcoin mainnet (UTXO-based) and Stacks (Clarity); standard EVM block explorers do not index these protocols. Bitcoin and Stacks explorers do not have formalized name-tag search endpoints like Etherscan.

Official deployments doc sourced from https://docs.stacks.co/learn/sbtc: identified sBTC SIP-010 token contract at SM3VDXK3WZZSA84XXFKAFAF15NNZX32CTSG82JFQ4.sbtc-token on Stacks mainnet, and Bitcoin peg UTXO at bc1prcs82tvrz70jk8u79uekwdfjhd0qhs2mva6e526arycu7fu25zsqhyztuy (multisig taproot address controlled by sBTC Signers).

No audit PDFs provided in protocol.audit_links (empty). Protocol.github is null per per-protocol context. Audit partnerships mentioned (Asymmetric Research, ImmuneFi) but URLs not yet sourced in this run.

GitHub repo at https://github.com/stacks-sbtc/sbtc identified but not yet walked for deployment artifacts (Foundry/hardhat-deploy/manual indexes). Repository confirmed active with v1.3.0 latest release (Apr 9, 2026).
Protocol operates across 2 settlement layers: Bitcoin (L1 UTXO) and Stacks (Clarity contracts). Single Stacks mainnet deployment. Bitcoin network is single chain (no per-chain variants of UTXO).
No factory contracts with child enumeration identified in current evidence. Emily API (https://docs.stacks.co/learn/sbtc) is a relay, not a factory. No pool or market enumeration performed.

Roles identified: sBTC token (SIP-010 fungible token), peg wallet (multisig taproot UTXO), sBTC Signer Set (governance/security, 15 initial signers), Emily API (relay/bridge infra). Signer set described as open-membership with 70% consensus requirement for deposit/withdrawal approval.
All addresses in admin_addresses[] trace to fetched URLs: Stacks docs, GitHub repo, integration guides (Medium, Hiro blog).

rationale.verdict: Discovery completed for sBTC protocol operating across Bitcoin L1 and Stacks L2. Primary contracts catalogued: Stacks SIP-010 token, Bitcoin multisig peg wallet, and supporting infrastructure (Emily API, Signer set). Crypto-economic security model relies on 15 Phase-1 signers with 70% consensus. No audit URLs sourced yet; GitHub repo structure not yet walked for deployment artifacts. Classification tentative pending D4 walkthrough and audit detail discovery.

evidence.shows: sBTC SIP-010 token contract address SM3VDXK3WZZSA84XXFKAFAF15NNZX32CTSG82JFQ4.sbtc-token on Stacks mainnet; Bitcoin peg UTXO at bc1prcs82tvrz70jk8u79uekwdfjhd0qhs2mva6e526arycu7fu25zsqhyztuy (multisig taproot); sBTC Signers signer set with 70% consensus requirement for deposits/withdrawals; Emily API role as bridge supervisor.
sBTC integration guide referencing SIP-010 standard and bridge mechanics; confirms Bitcoin finality and peg-in/peg-out flow.

sBTC mainnet launch December 17, 2024; 15 Phase-1 signers with 70% consensus; security partnerships with Asymmetric Research and ImmuneFi bug bounty program; decentralized signer set rather than single custodian.

Official repository for sBTC with GPL-3.0 license; contains Clarity contracts, signer/Emily infrastructure, bridge-website; v1.3.0 latest release (Apr 9, 2026); references deployer account SN3R84XZYA63QS28932XQF3G1J8R9PC3W76P9CSQS for Stacks contract deployment.
sBTC token contract principal: SM3VDXK3WZZSA84XXFKAFAF15NNZX32CTSG82JFQ4.sbtc-token; SIP-010 compliant; implicit trait implementation; transfer function primary interface.
Confirmed sBTC contract address SM3VDXK3WZZSA84XXFKAFAF15NNZX32CTSG82JFQ4.sbtc-token for mainnet integration; implicit SIP-010 implementation; 717 BTC bridged within 48 hours of launch (Dec 2024).

sBTC backed 1:1 by BTC; peg wallet maintained by decentralized signer set; 70% consensus requirement for operation approval; SIP-028 governance criteria for signer selection based on technical expertise, reliability, performance, decentralization; Phase 1 signer list public.

Design document describing sBTC as trustless two-way peg; open-membership signer set with economic security (integrated with Stacks consensus); decentralized approach vs. federated alternatives (L-BTC, R-BTC); Bitcoin finality for Stacks layer.

unknowns: D3: Audit URLs not yet sourced; protocol mentions Asymmetric Research and ImmuneFi partnerships but direct report/contract URLs require further discovery
D4: GitHub deployment artifacts (Clarity contracts, Emily/signer binaries, bridge-website code) not yet walked for commit SHAs and deployment addresses
D6-cap: Factory-pattern enumeration not applicable; sBTC is single-token bridge with no dynamic pool/market creation
D2-deployed-doc: No formal 'Deployed Contracts' index page identified yet; addresses sourced from concept/integration guides rather than dedicated deployment registry

protocol_metadata.about: sBTC is a decentralized, 1:1 Bitcoin-backed token on the Stacks Bitcoin L2 that enables users to bridge BTC in and out of smart contracts without custodial intermediaries. Deposits and withdrawals are secured by a decentralized set of 15 Phase-1 signers (with 70% consensus requirement) operating a multisig taproot UTXO on Bitcoin mainnet. The protocol launched December 17, 2024, featuring trustless peg mechanics, 100% Bitcoin finality, and the Emily API as a relay between users and signers.
